summary: Keep Zettlr from spawning duplicate windows/instances of external applications on export.

description.text:
### Description

It's great that Zettlr opens a document in your default PDF viewer on export, but when I'm updating a document and hitting export repeatedly to see the changes, I don't want it to spawn a new window/instance of the external application every time. Rather, if I have the PDF open in an existing external application window, I'd like Zettlr to refrain from launching duplicate windows/application instances.

Most PDF applications, for example, monitor changes on disk and will update the currently open window to show changes since the previous export, so there's no need to launch a new window every time a PDF export is complete.

### Proposed Changes

At one point, Zettlr did not do this, though I can't recall exactly when it started happening. Perhaps reverting whatever change created this behavior would be sufficient.

Alternatively, I'd suggest giving users a checkbox toggle, preferably in the export panel, but possibly tucked away in the settings menu, to enable or disable launching an external application window. Putting the toggle in the export panel would have the benefit of allowing users to easily choose on a case-by-case basis whether to launch a new instance of the external app. If implemented in this way, the checkbox should remember the user's last-used setting and stay toggled on/off until the user changes it.

### Caveats

A few:

1. It may still make sense for exports that use the `Temporary Directory` setting to launch a new window each time, given that these are not intended to create persistent files.
2. I think this partly has to do with the external application in use. Some PDF viewers avoid creating new instances and will deflect new open requests to the already-running application instance, which may avoid opening a new window every time. However, common default PDF viewer applications, such as Okular on Linux do not do this. Hence, it makes sense to give the user some control over the process.
3. I haven't discussed exports that open in Word Processors or other non-PDF applications, but I think the same general ideas should be applicable.

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

No

### Zettlr Version

Nightly

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Operating System Version

Manjaro (Rolling)

### Additional Information

Thank you, as always!

summary: feat: Switch Windows Code Signing to Azure Trusted Signing

description.text:
## Description

This PR switches the build pipeline from the expiring SSL.com code signing certificate to the new flow using the Azure Trusted Signing pipeline, using our Electron Builder CI workflow.

### Roadmap

In order to reduce the amount of potential nuisance for Windows users, the suggested roadmap for the switch will be as follows:

#### Step 1: Maintenance Release at the end of February

At the end of February, we should release a maintenance update for Zettlr including the most recent Electron binary to ensure it is as safe as possible, and prompt users to update. This will be the final release using the expiring certificate.

#### Step 2: Initial Release w/ New Certificate

Then, at the beginning of March, I want to release a new update that changes nothing except the certificate, and again prompt users to update. In case the new certificate has only basic initial trust, this means that users using a managed computer won't be able to update (one needs Admin access to override the Windows warning), so we have to rely on those users to "build up trust" with Microsoft.

#### Step 3: Resume Usual Workflow

Once the code signing works, and has been tested in the wild, resume the usual flow of updating the app once a few changes have built up.

> [!WARNING]
> This PR is a draft since it should only be merged **after the maintenance update** to reduce the potential amount of disruption. While my testing on a Windows 11 VM did not at all show the prompt that the binary is not trusted, I won't fully trust this, so I want to pull through with the change as initially planned.

## Changes

* Update to Electron Builder 26.0.1 to be able to use the new Azure Sign Options property
* Exchange the old `CSC` environment variables with new ones reflecting the usage of Azure
* Adapt a few breaking changes in the Electron Builder API

## Additional information

Some TODOs:

- [x] @kyaso I would like you to test out the Linux build. Electron Builder introduced a change that can be remedied by moving the `StartupWMClass` Desktop file entry as indicated into the `entry` property, but I have not been able to check if that will still work. It would be great if you could check and see if anything broke on the Linux Debian installer.
- [x] @nathanlesage Add the corresponding authentication variables as repository secrets ahead of the switch
- [ ] @nathanlesage Test the macOS installers produced by this build.

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: Windows 11 (non-activated VM); Access for the accounts tested via Azure CLI

summary: Internal links with title will not update after rename file.

description.text:
### Description

Setting Internal links use "Link with filename only" and title first for Link format.

1. Create two files 001.md and 002.md.
2. Create a internal link with title in file 002. That's say [[001| title001]].
3. Change filename of 001.md to 003.md.
4. Accept update internal link files. 
5. The internal link content in 002.md will not update to [[003| title001]].

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b59394b0-a23b-42a4-9fe5-add99cf9bf76

### Reproducing

1. Create two files 001.md and 002.md.
2. Create a internal link with title in file 002. That's say [[001| title001]].
3. Change filename of 001.md to 003.md.
4. Accept update internal link files. 
5. The internal link content in 002.md will not update to [[003| title001]].

### Zettlr version

3.4.1

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [x] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Sequoia 15.1.1

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Export option "Unlink internal links" does not affect the pdf output

description.text:
### Description

I have a .md file with several internal links between double brackets. I would like to preserve the text of the link in the pdf output, and get rid of the brackets.

However, despite ticking the "Unlink internal link" in the export option, the double brackets remain in the xelatex pdf export. Is this option only meant for other export formats?

### Reproducing

Export any .md file containing an internal link to xelatex pdf.

3.4.1

summary: Feature request: Make a direct button on the main screen to Graph view

description.text:

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)

The idea is to make it more accessible to the end user making it accessible direct from the main screen without going into two or three clicks.

summary: duplicate reference list export with custom .csl

description.text:
### Description

When using a custom .csl (citation style language) file, the bibliography/reference list is rendered twice in the exported document: Once in the default Chicago format, once in the custom style. This happens with multiple export profiles, e.g. the built-in markdown to "Simple PDF", "Plain Text", and "html" options, but not when exporting to XeLaTeX pdf or docx.

### Reproducing

Under preferences/citations, configure Zettlr with a citation database in bibtex format and a custom .csl (e.g. https://github.com/citation-style-language/styles/blob/master/acta-philosophica.csl). In a markdown file, add a citation and export the document with the stock "Simple pdf" pipeline.

### Zettlr version

3.4.1

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Operating System Version

summary: Copy-as-HTML doesn't turn plain links into actual links

description.text:
### Description

When copying some selected Markdown from a document as HTML (via `Ctrl+Alt+C` or via the context menu) that contains plain links in the form of `<https://www.google.com/>`, those links won't be turned into actual links.

Expected behavior: Turn them into `[https://www.google.com/](https://www.google.com/)` links (but as HTML).

### Reproducing

Simply try to copy some Markdown containing plain links as HTML.

### Zettlr version

3.4.1

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

summary: Export in HTML is putting my list of references twice. (Zotero + Bibtex)

description.text:
### Description

When I use the export tool and export to HTML (or Simple PDF), the document created contains twice my list of items. Once in the "Chicago Default Style" version installed by default by Zettlr, and another one in the style I defined with a .csl file in the software parameters.

It seems to me that that it has to do with the export parameters included in the "YAML / filters: citeproc" thing, but I'm no developer. 

### Reproducing

Using a custom .csl file and have citation keys enabled, then exporting into HTML or Simple PDF should repeat it. I'm using the "these-universite-sorbonne.csl" file.

### Zettlr version

3.4.1

### Installation Method

Flathub

### Installation Method (Other)
